reading.label: B
paragraphs: Omar works at a small *bakery* in the city. Every morning he makes bread for many customers. But the bakery needs *fresh* *flour* first. So Omar sends an *order* to his *supplier* by phone. He asks for twenty boxes of flour. The supplier says, "No problem. We can *deliver* today."

The supplier has a big *warehouse* near the city. Workers keep a lot of flour in the warehouse. First, they check the *stock* on the computer. They see there is enough flour for the order. Then a *driver* puts the heavy boxes on a *truck*.

The truck leaves early in the morning. The driver goes fast because Omar needs the flour. Soon the truck arrives at the bakery. Omar takes the boxes inside and says thank you. Now he can make fresh bread, and his customers are happy.

questions.question_en: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: How a bakery gets flour to make bread
How a man buys a new car
How to bake a cake at home
A long trip to another city
questions.correct_answer: How a bakery gets flour to make bread
questions.explanation_ar: النص يشرح كيف يصل الدقيق إلى المخبز لصنع الخبز.

questions.question_en: How does Omar send his order?
questions.explanation_ar: قال النص إن عمر يرسل الطلب للمورّد عبر الهاتف.

questions.question_en: How many boxes of flour does Omar ask for?
questions.question_ar: كم صندوقًا من الدقيق يطلب عمر؟
questions.explanation_ar: طلب عمر عشرين صندوقًا من الدقيق.

choices: A big place to keep things
questions.correct_answer: A big place to keep things

questions.question_en: What does the driver do after the workers check the stock?
questions.question_ar: ماذا يفعل السائق بعد أن يفحص العمال المخزون؟
choices: He puts the boxes on a truck
questions.correct_answer: He puts the boxes on a truck
questions.explanation_ar: بعد فحص المخزون، يضع السائق الصناديق على الشاحنة.

questions.question_en: Why does the driver go fast?
choices: Because the truck is very old
Because the city is far away
Because he is late for lunch
questions.explanation_ar: السائق يسرع لأن عمر بحاجة إلى الدقيق.

questions.question_en: Why are Omar's customers happy at the end?
questions.question_ar: لماذا يكون زبائن عمر سعداء في النهاية؟
choices: Because they can buy fresh bread
questions.correct_answer: Because they can buy fresh bread
questions.explanation_ar: الزبائن سعداء لأن الدقيق وصل فصار عمر يصنع خبزًا طازجًا.

vocab: a shop that makes and sells bread
Omar works at a small bakery.
a person or company that sends goods to a shop
The supplier sends flour to the bakery.
a request to buy or send goods
Omar sends an order for flour.
to take goods to a place
The truck can deliver the flour today.
a big building for keeping goods
They keep the flour in a big warehouse.
the goods that a shop or store keeps
They check the stock on the computer.
a person who drives a car or truck
The driver puts the boxes on the truck.
a big vehicle for carrying goods
The flour comes on a big truck.
a soft powder used to make bread
Omar makes fresh bread every day.

listening.transcript: Hi, I'm Khalid. I am a truck driver. Every day I take boxes from a big warehouse to many shops. This morning I have flour for a bakery in the city. The supplier gives me the order, and I put the boxes on my truck. I drive fast so the shop gets fresh flour early.

exercises.question_en: What is in the boxes this morning?
Where does Khalid take the flour?
exercises.explanation_ar: يأخذ خالد الدقيق إلى مخبز في المدينة.

exercises.question_en: Where do the boxes come from?

options: So the shop gets fresh flour early
exercises.explanation_ar: يسرع خالد ليصل الدقيق الطازج إلى المحل مبكرًا.
المورّد هو من يعطي خالد الطلب.
